intro.value: This transition is the branch split at onset. Eq. 11 still contains the trivial incoherent branch and the nonzero synchronized branch at the same time.
Eq. 12 is what you get only after restricting to the nonzero branch and dividing by the common factor r.

left.value: Eq. 11 is already a self-consistency equation for the locked population. But because r multiplies the entire right-hand side, the equation still allows the trivial solution r equals zero.
There are two possibilities. Either r is zero, giving the incoherent branch, or r is nonzero and can be divided out.
Eq. 12 is about the second case only. That is why the condition r greater than zero has to be stated explicitly.

steps.rightTitle: Recognize Eq. 12 as the Onset Equation

left.value: Once r has been divided out, the remaining equation is the branch condition that will later be analyzed as r approaches zero from the positive side.
That is the onset equation used to define the critical coupling.
